title: Checkpoints, Write-Ahead State, Crash Consistency, and Resume · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds versioned crash-consistent crawl checkpoint from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned crash-consistent crawl checkpoint while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explici…
objectives: Define which policy, seed, frontier, attempt, digest, report, and output states must survive and which can be recomputed.
Write bounded versioned state to a temporary file, sync by declared durability, validate, rename atomically, and retain recovery copies.
Return claimed or fetching items to an explicit retry state using attempt and idempotency rules after a crash.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Checkpoint Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 3036 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove state boundaries, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, policy-version admission, and resume reconciliation; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Checkpoint Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define which policy, seed, frontier, attempt, digest, report, and output states must survive and which can be recomputed. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Checkpoint Boundary, keep Checkpoint Boundary connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: Dumping every in-memory object is the safest checkpoint. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Checkpoint Boundary.

For commit to the mission model, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Checkpoint Boundary for versioned crash-consistent crawl checkpoint.
State the Checkpoint Boundary preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
